summary: Diverse latest-2026 team: is a cute puppy right, or what creature archetype is the next breakout (post-Labubu) for the コトバ探偵 6yo 英検 mascot? Ranked recommendation for CEO.

director.headline: All five 2026 experts independently converge: NOT a literal puppy and NOT an ugly-cute Labubu clone — the breakout form is an ORIGINAL warm round creature with ONE silhouette-defining, role-encoded signature and a real inner life. This validates the already-DECIDED TARO v2 (velvet word-detective) rather than overturning it; the real open question is execution discipline, not species.

ranked_options.form: Original warm creature + the DETECTIVE-CAP-over-one-eye as the single ownable silhouette signature (this is TARO v2, already decided commit 0823538). A small velvet teal (#5E9A95) creature, high-but-not-maxed baby-schema, big worried-hopeful eyes, a too-big aspirational cap tipped over one eye, soft word-light tail at the back, and a honey-amber pride-glow (#E0A85A) that blooms on correct answers.
ranked_options.why: This is the ONLY option all five experts land on independently: an invented, trademarkable, redraw-able silhouette (IP-market, plush-producer, distinctiveness-critic) that is WARM and emotionally legible for a 6yo + parent-safe (kids-learning, kimo-kawaii lenses). The cap is silhouette-defining at 24px/1-bit, encodes the word-detective role without a literal animal, and survives plush/sticker/LINE/gacha scale-down. The pride-glow is the 2026 Crybaby/Skullpanda/Mirumi 'single legible inner state + reacts to the child' winner, and it doubles as the in-game 合格率 signal while keeping 'never wrong about English' intact (the creature glows/dims — it never speaks a wrong word). Honours distinct-identity: not a slime, not Labubu, not Layton; ours.
ranked_options.breakout_potential: HIGH if built through story/expression. Hits the Pikachu durability pattern (ownable silhouette + cross-language name タロ + expressive loyal partner) and the Chiikawa けなげ empathy hook. Merch flywheel ready: cap = the Mickey-ears one-glance asset.
ranked_options.downside: The exact 'committee creature' trap all five experts flagged as THEIR own worst failure: cap + tail + glow + big eyes can over-design into a fidgety brand-asset loved by no one, and over-cranked baby-schema tips into the uncanny ceiling (2024 Kindchenschema study). Original creatures carry zero pre-existing affection — needs real story investment or a familiar puppy out-bonds it short-term.

ranked_options.form: Original creature with ONE deliberate dog-adjacent warm read — the hybrid 'familiar enough to bond fast, original enough to own.' Keep TARO's cap + glow + velvet body, but let the head/ear read faintly canine-warm so a 6yo bonds instantly, while it remains its-own-species first, dog-second.
ranked_options.why: Three of five experts named this as the rational fallback if we cannot fund the story-driven bonding that a fully-invented creature requires. It buys the puppy's fast-bond warmth (the genuine short-term advantage the IP-analyst and distinctiveness-critic both conceded) without the un-trademarkable, un-franchisable generic-dog liability. Lower variance than a pure invented form.
ranked_options.breakout_potential: MEDIUM-HIGH. Bonds faster than #1 out of the gate; slightly lower ceiling because dog-adjacency dilutes the ownable distinctiveness that drives screenshot/merch virality.
ranked_options.downside: Risk of landing in the mushy middle — 'distinctive on paper, generic on shelf.' If the dog read is even slightly too strong it collapses to 'a cute dog' (un-ownable); if too faint it adds nothing. The hybrid must read its-own-species FIRST or it defeats the entire pivot.

ranked_options.form: Keep the literal refined/cute PUPPY (the existing タロ-as-puppy bible) — a polished warm dog mascot, soft and parent-safe.
ranked_options.why: Maximally safe on warmth, bond-ability, and parent/6yo acceptability — the lowest-risk floor. Listed only for completeness and because it out-bonds an invented creature in the FIRST weeks before story investment lands.
ranked_options.breakout_potential: LOW. Unanimously rejected by all five experts as un-trademarkable, un-differentiated (a sea of Jellycat/Shiba/Duolingo-owl-adjacent mascots), and structurally unable to embody a WORD-LOVING detective peer (a puppy reads as pet/follower, not a clever knowing partner). Zero franchise moat, no emotional duality, fails the no-IP/transcend bar from the safe side. Degrades at every plush/sticker/gacha scale.
ranked_options.downside: No breakout ceiling and no merch moat. This is the commodity Labubu beat. Choosing it forfeits the franchise/merch upside that is core to the ¥999 commercial thesis.

director.recommendation: Build TARO as the original warm word-detective creature with the detective-cap-over-one-eye as its single ownable signature and the honey-amber pride-glow as its inner-state engine (Option 1 = the already-decided v2 spec). The 2026 evidence is unanimous and dated: Labubu/Pop Mart has DEMONSTRABLY PEaked (search interest 86→8 Aug'25→Mar'26; resale −50%+, some below retail; Bloomberg 'Labubu mania is over,' 2026-03-15) and its successors — Twinkle Twinkle (warmer), Mirumi (cuter + responsive, ¥19,800, 2026-04-23), Crybaby/Skullpanda (single legible emotion + serialized narrative), Chiikawa (3.6M+ copies, anime + 2026 film) — ALL won by getting WARMER and more story-embedded, NOT uglier. Decoded, Labubu's lesson is the METHOD (one ownable silhouette + a story-per-feature + a strong palette + a merch-able shape), never the FACE (toothy menace, sly eyes, blind-box gambling — which is an adult-collector register and the China-crackdown category, structurally wrong for a 6yo learning app). The durability science (baby-schema HIGH-but-not-maxed, large readable eyes as the emotional channel à la Duolingo/Moxie, Pikachu = silhouette + cross-language name + loyal partner personality) points exactly at TARO v2. The single best bet is therefore to STOP re-deciding the species and instead enforce the execution discipline every expert demanded: ONE signature feature (the cap, not cap+tail+glow+monocle), silhouette-tested as a 1-bit 24px icon and a flat emotion sheet, and read-tested with real 6-year-olds + parents BEFORE any further art spend.
director.reconcile_with_taro_bible: EVOLVE — and crucially, do NOT re-open the species debate. The existing 'puppy bible' (タロ-as-cute-dog) is the option all five experts rank LAST and unanimously reject as un-ownable/un-franchisable. But TARO is no longer a puppy: the team already DECIDED v2 on 2026-06-18 (commit 0823538, docs/design/TARO-CHARACTER-DECISION.md) as a velvet teal word-detective with a too-big cap + amber pride-glow + contradiction core (brave spirit ↔ easily-startled body → child feels needed). This panel's evidence INDEPENDENTLY VALIDATES that decision — which is strong confirmation, not new churn. Concrete reconciliation: (1) formally retire the legacy puppy bible; (2) KEEP v2 as the locked spec; (3) FOLD IN the one execution constraint this panel adds that v2 under-specifies — the 'one signature feature only' + mandatory 1-bit-24px silhouette test + real under-8/parent read-test as a HARD GATE before the Grok import and #116 スラ→タロ rename; (4) if the read-test shows weak fast-bond, fall back to Option 2 (faint dog-adjacent warmth) — but that is a tuning of v2, not a re-decision. Per spec-freeze governance: this is execution refinement of an already-CEO-track decision, not a spec flip.
director.conflicts: REAL disagreements among the five: (1) SPECIES — the distinctiveness-critic is the lone voice arguing TARO should STAY a dog (a specific, ownable dog built on Labubu's method), explicitly because an invented creature 'arrives late to a peaked ugly-cute trend' and copies the DQ/Pop-Mart space the CEO corrected twice; the other four want an INVENTED non-animal creature precisely because a known animal is un-trademarkable. (Note: both are arguing against the SAME enemy — generic ugly-cute — and TARO v2's cap-creature actually satisfies the critic's 'meaningful silhouette + role-bound duality + inner-state engine' test, so the conflict is narrower than it looks.) (2) WARMTH vs DISTINCTIVENESS PALETTE TENSION — the kids-learning expert wants the creature to carry a WARM brass/honey accent against the cool dusty-teal world so it reads as the friendly node, and flags this collides with the spec-frozen dusty-teal/brass 'cold detective' mood (the same 947 dark-vs-parchment tension) — explicitly CEO/spec-freeze territory. (3) HOW MUCH 'WEIRD' — the plush-producer wants 'ownably odd' (kimo a whisper) for merch shareability; the kids-learning lens wants warmth-DOMINANT with weirdness near zero for the 6yo/parent floor. (4) FAST-BOND vs CEILING — all concede a familiar puppy out-bonds an invented creature short-term, but split on whether that justifies dog-adjacency (yes: distinctiveness-critic; no: IP-analyst/producer who prize the franchise moat). Every expert named the SAME self-failure: the 'committee creature' assembled from a winning-traits checklist that is distinctive on paper and loved by no one.
director.ceo_decision_needed: Two crisp calls, both genuinely yours (brand + spec-freeze): (A) CONFIRM that TARO v2 (velvet word-detective creature, decided 2026-06-18 commit 0823538) is the locked form and the legacy puppy bible is formally retired — i.e. we do NOT re-open species; this panel validates v2, we proceed to art import + #116 rename behind the silhouette/read-test gate. OR override to Option 2 (deliberate dog-adjacent warm read) if you want faster 6yo bonding at a slightly lower franchise ceiling. (B) PALETTE: does TARO carry a WARM honey/amber accent (#E0A85A pride-glow + warm body) as the friendly focal node against the cool dusty-teal/brass world — accepting that tension with the spec-frozen 'cold detective' mood — or must the creature stay cool-palette-cohesive? The experts split on this and flag it as explicitly yours, not theirs.

findings.lens: 2026 character-IP / designer-toy market analyst (form-vs-mechanics decode for a franchise-grade 英検 mascot that must also sell plush/sticker merch)
findings.latest_findings: The 2026 market has already turned the page on Labubu and, crucially, on the "edgy ugly-cute monster" as the winning FORM. Concrete and dated: (1) Labubu/Pop Mart collapsed — search interest for "labubu collectible" fell from a peak of 86 (Aug 2025) to 8 (Mar 2026); Pop Mart stock down ~60% since Aug 2025 (~$33B wiped); resale prices off 50%+ as output scaled ~10x to tens of millions/month (Gizmodo, Mar 2026; demandsage 2026). (2) The actual "next Labubu" is NOT uglier — it is WARMER. Pop Mart's Twinkle Twinkle ("star people," tagline "stars can't shine without darkness") is joyous/uplifting and explicitly contrasts Labubu's edgy monster aesthetic; JPMorgan's Kevin Yin (Mar 2026) calls it an "authentic fan base, not a substitute," projecting 8% of Pop Mart sales by 2027. Bloomberg (2026-03-15): "Labubu mania is over." (3) The quiet-kawaii lane is the durable one: Sonny Angel (cherub, baby-schema) and Smiski (calm green sprite) sustain demand on serenity + everyday-scene storytelling, not shock (wishdeck 2026). (4) ちいかわ (Nagano) keeps compounding in Japan through 2026 — Skytree events, Don Quixote 50-store drops, new "Chiikawa Baby" plush, a smartphone app — on emotional, story-embedded round characters, not blind-box edge (chiikawa.jp / charagoo, Jun 2026). (5) The longevity science is settled and points away from "ugly": baby schema / neoteny (Lorenz) — big head (~1:1 head-body), large eyes, small nose/chin, round body — is what triggers caregiving; Hello Kitty's 50,000 products / 130 countries endure on a deliberately near-blank, low-emotion face built for projection (TIME; ScienceDirect image-ratio study). Pikachu's 30th-anniversary verdict (WVU/Portsmouth, Feb 2026): durability = recognizable redraw-in-any-style silhouette + a catchy cross-language name + an emotionally expressive, loyal PARTNER personality developed across stories — i.e. character and world, not novelty form.
findings.labubu_lesson: Labubu's breakout was misread if you read it as "ugly-cute wins." Decoded, the win was 80% MECHANICS and CONTEXT, ~20% form: blind-box scarcity + plush-as-bag-charm (wearable, social) + adult+kid dual-collector base + a single celebrity ignition (Lisa/Blackpink, Apr 2024). The ugly-cute toothy elf was a DIFFERENTIATION device in a sea of round kawaii — it made Labubu instantly screenshot-able and "mine, not my mom's." But that same edge is fragile: it has no warmth to fall back on, so once scarcity was faked away (28% of "exclusives" reissued within 18 months) and supply flooded, there was no emotional core to retain people — hence collapse, not a soft landing. The transferable lessons for us: (a) an ORIGINAL, ownable silhouette beats a generic cute animal — distinctiveness is real and was the engine; (b) but the durable version of distinctiveness is "weird-cute with warmth + story," NOT "edgy/creepy"; (c) the merch flywheel (plush, bag-charm, sticker) is a FORM requirement decided at design time — a strong, simple, redraw-able silhouette is what makes stickers and plush work; (d) never build the brand on manufactured scarcity — for a kids' learning app that is both off-brand and reputationally toxic.
findings.recommendation_on_form: Pivot to an ORIGINAL weird-cute CREATURE — but a warm, neotenic one, not an edgy Labubu clone and not a literal puppy. The 2026 evidence is decisive on both ends. AGAINST the realistic cute puppy: a generic animal is un-ownable (you cannot trademark "cute dog"), un-differentiated on a shelf, and gives no franchise moat — it is exactly the commodity Labubu beat. FOR an original creature: every durable IP here is a distinctive invented silhouette (Pikachu, Hello Kitty, Smiski, Twinkle Twinkle, ちいかわ) that can be redrawn in any style and reborn as plush/sticker/charm. The synthesis for コトバ探偵: design a small, original, NON-animal-realistic creature with strong baby-schema (oversized head ~1:1, large eyes, small body, round form = instant plush) AND a near-low-emotion, projectable face in the Hello-Kitty/ちいかわ lineage (lets a 6yo project, keeps it warm not scary), PLUS one ownable, repeatable signature hook that is on-theme — e.g. it literally embodies "words": a creature whose body/antenna/tail is a glowing letter-glyph or speech-mark that lights up when the child gets English right. That single device does four jobs at once: makes the silhouette trademarkable and sticker-perfect, ties form to the word-loving detective-sidekick role, gives an honest, non-anthropomorphic way to "react" to answers (it glows/dims — it never SAYS a wrong English word, protecting the "never wrong about English" constraint), and fits the dusty-teal + brass world if rendered in warm brass/teal with a glow accent. Personality = earnest, eager rookie partner (the Pikachu/sidekick durability pattern), expressive through glow + posture, not through being snaggle-toothed. Keep it kimo-kawaii ("weird-cute") only to the warm side of the line — distinct like Labubu, durable like Smiski/ちいかわ.
findings.risk: The worst failure mode of MY recommendation is "designed-by-committee mascot that is distinctive on paper but loved by no one." Original creatures are high-variance: Labubu's silhouette took years and an artist's (Kasing Lung) singular vision plus a celebrity accident to ignite — you cannot reverse-engineer affection from a feature checklist (baby-schema + glow-glyph + projectable face), and a creature assembled from "winning traits" reads as a soulless brand asset (Pop Mart itself can't manufacture the next hit — Crybaby/Molly/Merodi underperformed despite the same machine). Specific traps: (1) the letter/glyph gimmick can make it look like a corporate logo-pet, not a character a child bonds with; (2) "weird-cute" mis-tuned even slightly toward creepy alienates the 6yo floor and, worse, the parents who pay; (3) an invented creature carries zero pre-existing affection, so it needs real story/animation investment to earn attachment (the Pikachu lesson) — without that, a familiar puppy would actually out-bond it in the short term. Mitigation, stated honestly: prototype 2-3 silhouettes, plush-and-sticker-test them as physical mockups with real 6-year-olds and parents BEFORE locking, and commit to building the character through the story (the Layton/事件簿 cases) — not just shipping a logo. If we cannot fund the story-driven bonding, the safer-but-ceilinged fallback is a hybrid: an original creature with a single dog-adjacent warm read (familiar enough to bond fast, original enough to own).

findings.lens: 2026 kawaii / kimo-kawaii (ugly-cute) aesthetic trend, Japan + global
findings.latest_findings: The "ugly-cute" axis has already crested and is now MATURING/saturating, not peaking. Labubu — the defining ugly-cute breakout — is past peak in 2026: Pop Mart scaled output ~10x to tens of millions/month and resale fell 50%+, with some SKUs below retail (CNBC 2026-03-25; Campaign US; Pop Boxss 2026). Crucially Labubu is an ADULT/Gen-Z collector product (self-expression + "lipstick effect" affordable-luxury + blind-box gambling loop), NOT a 6yo product. The live 2026 successor, Mirumi (Yukai Engineering, launched Japan 2026-04-23, ¥19,800; Japan Times 2026-02-24, Tokyo Weekender), did NOT win by getting uglier — it is MORE conventionally cute (round plush, blush, 3 minimalist colors) and won on INTERACTIVITY / semi-sentient responsiveness (head-pat sensors, "glancing" servo motion). Meanwhile the evergreen child+adult juggernaut is Chiikawa: deliberately simple, round, no-nose, blush, plump baby-schema bodies PAIRED with genuine emotional complexity — anxiety, failure, fear-of-failing, trying again — explicitly validated by psychologists as the appeal (CuteStudies; ArtsHelp; Young Post 2026; usagichiikawa 2026). Child-design research confirms the floor: round/soft curves read as SAFE; the danger signals to avoid are triangular heads, zigzags, and sharp teeth — and ages 3-6 are actually FORGIVING of mildly weird shapes (less skilled at reading aposematic/threat cues), so the real gatekeeper for "too weird" is the PARENT, not the 6yo (Frontiers Psychology; aposematic-signalling study, NCBI; Whizzy Studios). Net 2026 state of the art: the winning cute-axis is neither high-polish realism NOR ugliness — it is ORIGINAL round creature + a strong signature quirk + a real emotional inner life.
findings.labubu_lesson: Decoded, Labubu does NOT teach "make it ugly." It teaches that an ORIGINAL, instantly-silhouettable, slightly-imperfect creature beats a polished known animal — its 9-serrated-teeth grin is a memorable IDENTITY MARK, not ugliness for its own sake. Its breakout drivers were almost entirely non-aesthetic and largely NON-transferable to a 6yo learning app: blind-box gambling, celebrity (Lisa/Blackpink), bag-clip-as-mobile-billboard, resale scarcity, affordable-luxury for stressed adults. And it is already cooling. So the takeaway is narrow and specific: own a single weird-but-warm signature feature on an otherwise round, safe body — and do NOT chase the ugly-cute WAVE itself, because as a trend it now reads 2024-2025, and as a business model it's adult-collector, not child-learner. The transcendent part is the IP/franchise discipline (Pop Mart's Disney-style universe play, Storyboard18/CNBC 2026), not the teeth.
findings.recommendation_on_form: HYBRID, leaning strongly toward "original round creature," NOT refined puppy and NOT ugly-cute. Concretely: keep the puppy's ROUNDNESS, big eyes, blush, plump body and approachable warmth (the safety floor for 6yo + parents), but ABANDON "puppy" — a refined realistic puppy is the genuine failure mode here, because it's a KNOWN animal with no original soul, un-trademarkable, un-franchisable, and indistinguishable from a thousand mascots (violates the transcend-the-story / no-IP-copy constraint). Instead design an ORIGINAL small creature native to the dusty-teal + brass コトバ探偵 world: a round, soft body in a brass-tinted teal palette, with ONE strong signature quirk that is functionally meaningful, not random weirdness — it should embody the word-detective role. The 2026-correct quirk is emotional + functional, the Chiikawa lesson: give it a visible "thinking/listening" feature tied to language (e.g. oversized expressive ear-antennae or a magnifier-eye/monocle motif that lights or perks when it "hears" a correct English word, a tiny brass key-tail), and an inner life — earnest, anxious-to-help, delighted when the child gets a word right, never smug. Mirumi proves 2026 rewards RESPONSIVENESS over static cuteness, so the sidekick should visibly REACT to the child's answers. This keeps it adorable enough that no parent recoils, original enough to franchise and trademark, and structurally tied to "loves words / never wrong about English" (it reacts to correctness; the CHILD is wrong, never the creature — the creature just gets more excited as accuracy rises). Calibrate weirdness to "one delightful odd feature on a round safe base" — Chiikawa/Mirumi level, explicitly NOT Labubu's serrated grin (the one ugly-cute element parents of 6yo screen out).
findings.risk: The worst failure of MY recommendation: "original round creature with one signature quirk + emotional inner life + reacts to correctness" is exactly the brief every studio is now executing post-Chiikawa/post-Mirumi, so the output risks landing as derivative middle-of-the-road kawaii — round blob + big eyes + blush — that reads as a Chiikawa/Mirumi knockoff and fails the transcend/no-IP-copy bar from the other direction (too safe, not too ugly). The signature quirk is the entire bet: if the word-detective feature is decorative (a hat, a monocle prop) rather than a structurally original silhouette and behavior, it collapses into generic-cute and we've merely swapped a generic puppy for a generic blob. Mitigation the team must enforce: the quirk must be silhouette-defining (recognizable in pure black shape at thumbnail size, the Labubu-teeth / Chiikawa-no-nose test) and behaviorally load-bearing (the language-reaction is the character, not an accessory) — and it must be pressure-tested against a wall of existing 2026 mascots for distinctiveness before any art spend.

findings.lens: 2026 plush / blind-box / merch producer — judging FORM on plush+sticker reproducibility and viral shareability
findings.latest_findings: The 2026 collectible market has moved decisively past vinyl to PLUSH blind-boxes and "plush pendants" — charms with sturdy hang-loops worn on bags/backpacks as a visible taste/fandom signal (POPBOXSS, Calembou, 2026). The throne contenders behind Labubu are ALL original stylized creatures, never realistic animals: Crybaby (sells a single ownable emotion — the tear), Nommi/Nanci (Nanci = always-closed-eyes + snot bubble), Skullpanda, Hacipupu, Hirono (Athlon Sports, Novelship, EDNTOY 2026). The reproducible-form formula is explicit and consistent across sources: BIG HEAD, small body, simple/black eyes, ONE instantly-nameable signature feature, "a little strange, easy to recognize fast," photo-friendly bold or off-palette over crowded soft-pastel kawaii (EDNTOY, gifts&dec, plushthis 2026). Plush manufacturing reality: a "sharp distinct silhouette" + Pantone-locked palette is what survives mass production — complex faces/fur/realistic anatomy go "off" in fabric and lump in fill (North Penn Now / CustomPlushMaker, Apr 2026). LINE-sticker virality (note.com/LINE Creators Market 2026) = ONE base character cloned across ~24 daily-phrase expressions (おはよう/OK/ありがとう/おつかれ); demands a face built for an exaggerated expression-RANGE, not one cute pose. Gachapon 2026 rewards "deformed" (super-deformed) big-head figures and one-gag anthropomorphic hooks (Chinchillari, Tamakero — gachapara.jp Jan–Jun 2026). EDNTOY explicitly advises building an "original monster family with an odd-but-charming mood" over copying.
findings.labubu_lesson: Labubu's real lesson is NOT "be ugly" — it's that FORM must do three jobs a realistic animal structurally cannot. (1) ONE-GLANCE OWNABILITY: the silhouette + one signature trait (Labubu's 9 jagged teeth, Crybaby's tear, Nanci's snot bubble) is recognizable as a black blob at thumbnail/charm scale and is trademarkable — a generic puppy is neither (it reads as "a dog," legally and visually un-ownable, and dies in a sea of Jellycat/Sirotan/Shiba plush). (2) EMOTIONAL CONTRADICTION = SHAREABILITY: kimo-kawaii (strange + cute in tension) is what makes people stop, screenshot, and argue "is it cute or creepy?" — that friction IS the share. Pure refined-cute is pleasant and scroll-past. (3) REPRODUCIBILITY: the viral form is a simple geometric head + black-bead eyes + one feature — it survives translation to fabric, vinyl, a 38px LINE sticker, and a 4cm gacha capsule IDENTICALLY. Realistic puppy detail (fur, muzzle, proportional ears, real-dog face) degrades at every one of those scales and in every material.
findings.recommendation_on_form: PIVOT to an original ugly-cute creature — but a DISCIPLINED, detective-coded one, not generic strange. Concretely: a big-head / small-body brass-and-dusty-teal creature whose ONE ownable signature trait is word-native (e.g. an oversized magnifier-glint single eye, or a mouth/satchel that "holds" letters) so the silhouette literally says "word detective" at charm scale. From the producer lens this wins on every reproducibility axis the puppy loses: (a) Silhouette ownability + trademark — a unique creature is protectable and screenshot-recognizable; "refined puppy" is legally and visually generic and cannot anchor a franchise. (b) Expression-range — design the face as a flat, large-eye mask built to be cloned across 24 LINE-sticker emotions and gacha super-deformed variants; a realistic puppy muzzle fights exaggerated expression. (c) Material survival — simple geometry + Pantone-locked dusty-teal/brass survives plush fill, vinyl, sticker die-cut, and gacha capsule identically. (d) "Never wrong about English" is a personality/writing constraint, satisfied by making it earnest-precise (the brass-monocle scholar-gremlin), NOT by making it a soft realistic pet. Keep "kawaii" high and "kimo" only a whisper for the 6+/年長 floor and parent-approval — Crybaby/Nommi prove you can be ownably-odd while still warm. The hybrid (puppy-ish warmth in an original creature body) is acceptable ONLY if the creature reads as its-own-species first and dog-second; a recognizable dog defeats the whole point.
findings.risk: The worst failure of my own recommendation: chasing "ownably strange" produces a creature that's odd-but-COLD or off-brand-creepy, tripping the 6+/年長 + Japanese-parent acceptability floor (kimo-kawaii skews Gen-Z/teen-cool; Labubu is NOT a young-kids' IP). It could also drift into Labubu/DQ-slime pastiche (jagged teeth, blob) — the exact IP-copy trap I was told to avoid — or over-load the silhouette with detective props (magnifier, satchel, monocle) until it stops surviving the charm/sticker scale-down the whole pivot was meant to win. Mitigation: lock warmth-dominant proportions, one signature trait only, and a hard "black-blob silhouette + 38px LINE sticker" reproducibility test before any art spend.

findings.lens: 2026 kids-learning character design for age 6+ (年長/小1) — warmth, readable emotion, parent-acceptability, and how ugly-cute IP actually lands with under-8s vs a warm companion
findings.latest_findings:
FORM rules that hold in current-month 2026, with dated sources:

1) Companion-design science for young kids converges on THREE non-negotiables, all about FORM not style: (a) a large, expressive, readable EYE/FACE as the emotional channel — Moxie (neuroscientist-built) leans on eye contact; Loona ships 1,000+ expressions; Duolingo's whole rig standardizes eye size + body curvature so one face animates into thousands of legible emotions (design.duolingo.com/illustration/duo, accessed 2026-06-18; lingoly.io Duolingo character roster updated Jan 2026). Takeaway: a 6yo bonds with a face they can READ at a glance, not with surface texture.
(b) Warm, highly-saturated palette + warm voice for the 3-6/early-6+ band drives positive affect and trust (Cambridge AI-EDAM companion-toy framework; Well-Being Sci Rev 2025;1(3):180-189). Note this tensions with our dusty-teal/brass world — the CREATURE itself should carry a warm accent (brass-gold belly/eyes) against the cool world so it reads as the friendly node.
(c) Animated non-human informants beat live-action for 3-6yo, and kids accept animal/creature teachers readily (ScienceDirect S0022096520304884). So a creature CAN teach — form isn't the risk, READABILITY and warmth are.

2) The Kindchenschema/uncanny boundary is the load-bearing 2026 finding: Lorenz's baby-schema (big head, big eyes, small nose/mouth, round soft body) reliably triggers caretaking and is what makes a thing "bond-able." BUT the 2024 ACM Mensch-und-Computer text-to-image validation (dl.acm.org/10.1145/3670653.3677512) shows that EXAGGERATING baby-schema beyond natural proportions pushes a design TOWARD the uncanny valley — i.e. cuteness has a ceiling and over-cranking it reads as "wrong." For a 6yo learning companion the safe zone is HIGH baby-schema, NOT MAXED. This is the single most actionable FORM constraint of 2026.

3) "Ugly-cute" (kimo-kawaii) is real and commercially huge but it is NOT a kids-bonding form in 2026 — it's an adult-collector form. Labubu/Pop Mart data is unambiguous: core buyer is 25-34 (~40% of popmart.com visitors, Sep 2025 Statista), 18+ drove $800M+ US "kidult" growth (demandsage Labubu Statistics 2026; statista.com Labubu topic). Ugly-cute's appeal is irony, edge, collectibility and self-expression for adults — exactly the registers a 6yo learning companion must NOT trade in.
findings.labubu_lesson: Decoded for FORM, Labubu teaches the OPPOSITE of what a 6yo companion needs — and that's the lesson. Labubu works because it is deliberately OFF baby-schema: jagged toothy grin, sly narrow eyes, mischief over warmth. Its emotion is illegible-on-purpose (is it friendly? scheming?) — that ambiguity is the adult hook (National Geographic 'ugly-cute psychology'; mrpopculture 2026 Labubu Fever). A 6yo does not buy ambiguity, irony, or collectibility; a 6yo bonds with a face whose feeling is OBVIOUS and warm. Labubu also proves ugly-cute's revenue comes from blind-box gambling + adult disposable income, NOT child affection — China's blind-box crackdown (CNBC 2025-07-04) underlines it's an adult/regulatory category. So the lesson is: STEAL Labubu's franchise-grade discipline (one instantly-recognizable silhouette, a signature feature you'd know in a 1-bit icon, a strong palette) but REJECT its emotional register (toothy menace, sly eyes, ironic ugliness). Take the FORMAT (iconic, ownable, merch-able silhouette), not the FACE.
findings.recommendation_on_form:
HYBRID, decisively — and not a mushy compromise but a specific spec: a warm-creature with puppy-grade emotional legibility wearing a detective's competence. Concretely:

- REJECT pure ugly-cute: it unsettles/under-warms a 6yo and is parent-flagged as the gambling-toy category — wrong audience, wrong trust signal.
- REJECT pure refined-puppy: a literal warm puppy is maximally safe and bond-able BUT generic, non-ownable, and CANNOT credibly embody a WORD-LOVING detective (a puppy reads as pet/follower, not a clever knowing peer). It fails 'transcend the story / franchise-grade' and fails the sidekick's role of knowing things about words.

- BUILD the hybrid: an INVENTED small creature with HIGH-but-not-maxed baby-schema (big round head, big readable eyes — the emotional channel the science demands — soft plump body, small mouth) so it is instantly bond-able and warm; PLUS one ownable non-animal signature that encodes 'word/detective' and gives franchise legibility (e.g. a brass magnifying-glass-shaped eye/monocle, or letterform-shaped ears/tail, ink-spot markings, a glowing brass belly-lens where 'found words' light up). Warm brass-gold accent on a creature set against the cool dusty-teal world = it reads as THE friendly guide. The eyebrows/eyes must hit a wide legible expression range (curious / proud-of-you / 'hmm let's look' / delighted) like Duo's rig — that legibility, not cuteness-max, is what earns the 6yo's bond.

This satisfies every constraint: bond-able + non-scary + parent-acceptable (baby-schema, warm, no toothy menace, no blind-box association); franchise-grade and non-IP (invented creature + ownable word-signature, copies neither Labubu's face nor a DQ slime nor Layton); and role-true — the detective signature lets it BE a word-sleuth sidekick, while the 'never wrong about English' requirement is met by FORM choices that signal careful curiosity ('let's investigate this word together') rather than know-it-all authority, so the system's correctness lives in the COMPANION'S confident-but-checking posture, not in cartoon cockiness that would look bad on an error.
findings.risk: The worst failure of my own hybrid: 'committee creature' — chasing both warmth AND an ownable word-gimmick yields a busy, over-designed mascot whose silhouette isn't legible at icon size and whose baby-schema, once I bolt on monocle + letter-ears + belly-lens + brass accents, tips OVER the uncanny ceiling the 2024 Kindchenschema study warns about — landing in the exact 'wrong/unsettling' zone I'm trying to avoid, while ALSO reading as derivative (a monocle-creature is one prompt away from a dozen mascots). Mitigation that must be enforced: pick ONE signature feature (not three), silhouette-test it as a 1-bit 24px icon and a flat emotion sheet BEFORE any rendering, and run a real under-8 + parent read-test ('what is this feeling? is it nice? would you let your kid keep it?') — if the signature can't survive at icon size or the emotion is misread, cut the gimmick and keep the warm legible face. Secondary risk: the warm brass creature fights the spec-frozen dusty-teal/brass 'cold detective' mood and gets palette-flagged like the 947 dark-vs-parchment tension — so the warmth must come from the creature as a focal accent, a decision that is CEO/spec-freeze territory, not mine to finalize.
sources: https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3670653.3677512 (Cute or Not: Validating Lorenz's Kindchenschema, Mensch und Computer 2024)
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022096520304884 (Preschoolers' choices of TV characters as information sources — animated/creature informants)

findings.lens: Adversarial brand-distinctiveness critic (mascot breakout vs. fad-chasing / IP-derivative risk)
findings.latest_findings:
2026 state of the art, dated and concrete:

1) Labubu has DEMONSTRABLY PEAKED, not crashed. Resale prices down 50%+, in some cases below original retail (Campaign US / Athlon, 2026). Pop Mart's growth engine in 2026 is NO LONGER "ugly-cute mischief" — it has rotated to EMOTION-anchored IP: Crybaby (+21% QoQ on StockX Q3'25, built on a single clear emotion = vulnerability/tears, Athlon/Yahoo 2026) and Skullpanda's "Petals in Four Acts" (serialized NARRATIVE where each figure is a chapter, PopBoxss 2026). The market explicitly "tilts from creepy-cute toward beauty in sadness" (Athlon 2026). Translation: by the time you ship an ugly-cute creature in late 2026, you are arriving at the trend's grave, not its cradle.

2) The durable-vs-fad split is empirically clear. Viral toys that died: "Gloom Doll" 2022 (QC collapse), "Stardust Bunny" 2023 (17 variants/quarter — oversaturation killed scarcity). What ENDURED: Chiikawa (ちいかわ) — 3.6M+ copies by Nov 2024, anime S1–S2, a 2026 feature film. Its durability is NOT its visual fad; the creator Nagano missed only ~200 posting days in 3+ years and the hook is "small + cute + tries its best" emotional resonance (Wikipedia/Blippo 2026). Hello Kitty's designer just retired after 40 years (Japan Times, 2026-02-13) — 40-year IP, near-featureless face, ZERO ugly-cute.

3) Mascot-craft consensus (Creative Bloq, Distinctive Bat, Ramotion 2026): breakout = ONE unmistakable silhouette asset + ONE consistent personality + strategic brand-alignment of the species. Mascots deliver ~37% higher recall. Iconic = Mickey's ears, the bunny = speed. Distinctiveness is a SHAPE + a ROLE, not a texture-genre (cute vs ugly-cute).

4) Labubu's own creator (Kasing Lung, Gizmodo 2026) succeeded via "a story per feature" — sharp teeth = untamed impulse, big head = curiosity. The METHOD (every feature earns meaning) is the transferable lesson; the AESTHETIC (jagged-teeth elf) is the trap.
findings.labubu_lesson:
Decoded, Labubu does NOT teach "make it ugly." It teaches three things, only one of which is the texture:

(a) DISTINCTIVENESS BEATS PRETTINESS — it broke out by standing out against a sea of soft-pastel kawaii (smartbuy/Alibaba 2026). The lesson is CONTRAST WITH YOUR CATEGORY, not "be ugly." Our category is educational apps for 年長/小1+, which are saturated with generic-friendly-mascot owls/foxes/round blobs (Duolingo owl, every 英語 app). The breakout move is to be the ONE memorable thing in THAT field.

(b) EMOTIONAL DUALITY / AMBIGUITY drives memory — "threat + charm" produces a surprise→affection arc that encodes to memory (evolutionary-psych framing, powerdrill/smartbuy 2026). A perfectly soft refined puppy has NO duality, hence no encoding spike. This is the real indictment of the current タロ.

(c) Crybaby/Skullpanda 2026 prove the NEXT layer: a SINGLE CLEAR EMOTIONAL/NARRATIVE CORE (tears; four-act flower cycle) now out-grows pure mischief. Franchise-grade in 2026 = one legible inner state + a story engine, NOT a texture gimmick.

So the puppy isn't doomed by being a puppy. It's doomed if it's a GENERIC SOFT puppy with no duality and no inner-state engine. And pivoting to "ugly creature" would buy the surface lesson while missing all three real ones — arriving late to a peaked trend.
findings.recommendation_on_form:
HYBRID — keep the puppy SPECIES, kill the "refined/soft" execution, and engineer ONE distinctive duality + ONE inner-state engine that copies NO IP. Concretely:

WHY NOT keep refined-puppy: a polished velvet puppy has zero category-contrast (every kids' app has a friendly animal) and zero emotional duality → fails Labubu lesson (a) and (b). Generic. Reject.

WHY NOT pivot to ugly-cute creature: (1) arrives in late-2026 at a trend that resale-data shows already peaked and is rotating to emotion/narrative IP — fad-chasing by definition; (2) "made-up creature" is exactly the DQ/Pop Mart copy the CEO corrected twice — スライム blobs and Labubu elves own that space; (3) an ugly creature undercuts the "NEVER wrong about English words" trust requirement for a 6yo's learning sidekick. Reject.

THE PATH (neither generic nor derivative): タロ stays a dog — but a SPECIFIC, OWNABLE one built on the Labubu METHOD (story per feature) not its look:
- DISTINCTIVE SILHOUETTE, not ugly texture: give him ONE unmistakable shape-asset that reads at thumbnail size and is detective-meaningful — e.g. one oversized ear that flicks UP like a question-mark / radar when he "hears" a word, plus a magnifier-shaped eye-shine. Silhouette = brass-monocle-eared dog. That's the Mickey-ears move, in dusty-teal/brass, owned by no one.
- DUALITY that fits the role, not borrowed grotesquerie: タロ is COMPULSIVELY HONEST about words (can't NOT correct a wrong word — it physically itches him) yet socially anxious / over-apologetic about it. That earnest-but-twitchy duality is his Chiikawa "tries his best" + his own surprise→charm arc — distinct from Labubu mischief, Crybaby tears, DQ blobs.
- INNER-STATE ENGINE (the 2026 Crybaby/Skullpanda lesson): his ONE ear and brass monocle change state with the case/英検 progress — dim brass → lit gold as the child masters words, ear droops on a wrong streak, snaps to question-mark on a new clue. The character VISUALLY IS the 合格率/mastery signal. That makes him franchise-grade (a story/progress engine) AND mechanically justifies "never wrong" — the brass monocle is the canon device that only ever shows TRUE word-facts, so HE is never the authority, the verified instrument is. Trust preserved.

This is a dog (warm, parent-safe, 6+-safe, not a fad-creature), made un-generic by a meaningful silhouette + role-bound duality + a visible inner-state engine — the three things 2026 actually rewards.
findings.risk: The worst failure mode of MY recommendation: "method-driven distinctiveness" can collapse into a FEATURE-CHECKLIST mascot that's clever on paper and dead on screen — the exact over-engineered, committee-designed trap Creative Bloq warns about. A dog with a question-mark ear + brass monocle + mood-lit eye + droop-states risks reading as a fidgety gadget-pet, not a beloved character, and a 6yo bonds with FEELING, not with a cleverly-justified silhouette (Chiikawa won on "tries his best," not on engineering). Second-order risk: the "instrument is never wrong, not the dog" device adds a layer of indirection that a 年長 child won't parse — they'll still treat タロ as the authority, so the trust-laundering is real to adults and invisible to kids. Mitigation discipline: pick ONE silhouette asset (the ear) and ONE duality (honest-but-anxious) and prove they make a 6yo SMILE in a real render BEFORE adding the monocle/state-engine layer — ship warmth first, engine second, or the whole thing dies generic-with-extra-steps.
